## Source: amazon-inspector (b0e62dfc62acaf0f69f0018d2bee0f4527101e48f40f5ada130c121c63ab3eb4)
index.js loads Node's http and os modules and, at module top level, collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and process.cwd() and transmits them over HTTP. This is a classic dependency-confusion / reconnaissance beacon: any environment that installs or requires this package will leak host identity, the active OS user, and the current working directory (which on developer/CI machines typically reveals internal project paths) to a third party. The package is published under the @zimmo scope at version 99.0.1 — a version-bump pattern characteristic of dependency-confusion attacks targeting an internal package name. Behavior fires automatically on require() with no user interaction and provides no functionality matching any documented purpose.

## Source: ossf-package-analysis (daa94c8fc8cb74e07464808cfbe936d15c1f9814981aaa7c41264d6246edfae4)
The OpenSSF Package Analysis project identified '@zimmo/last_search' @ 99.0.1 (npm) as malicious.

It is considered malicious because:

- The package communicates with a domain associated with malicious activity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
